I've been playing through side scenarios, and did Elworth's Returnee, the Helot: Catena story. It has Resonance to upgrade the Explorers, and the rules for Resonance on the page say that clearing a chapter gives the materials to upgrade the Resonance. However, I did not get any after clearing the full story. So either:

1) I should have gotten them and didn't
or
2) Since this was an event, they were gained in an alternative way. I see that I can upgrade them by spending Rose Script now, which makes me think this is the case. But if that's true, then the Rules tab needs to be changed.

Already explained in another post that that games are meant to be played for the rewards, but it goes even further than that.

Imagine trying to show Morimens to a new potential player and the person asks:
-How do I build my account?

And the answer is that account progression not only 100% tied with playing Traphase, but also getting a good rank (same applies for P-Dive and HSR) because you need Dreamsparks and Dreamshards for D-Tide and Arc 2.

I really like this game, but current situation simply toxic. I don't believe I can show Morimens, explain how it's currently working and convince ANYONE to play the game.
By the time soulforge becomes relevant you have a solid enough team to at least get a decent score on those though...
Hello! I have extracted Silver Prime via PC through Steam client several times before V2.5 , but I can't get the associated rewards from the event "Comms Shifting" (Helot skin and 1 Luminous Core)
I'm guessing they haven't implemented a look back or something to check. I also bought things before on steam and it wasn't unlocked. I bought a new package and it unlocked.

CONCLUSION:

BIAV is making pressure to and punishing its main customers, AKA PVE players, who don't want to play a bonus mode, AKA Traphase, FOR NO REASON, while lowering the attractive of the bonus mode.

FACTS:

1. Expiring rewards is a disguised form of punishments which exploits the human nature of people hating loss.

2. it's reasonable for Games,  using such punishments to pressure players to play more, therefore players possibly pay more. 

3. PVE is the SOLE PRIMARY MODE, which the business model of Morimens is based on.

4. There are few monetization point‌s in PVP mode in Morimens. Thus "PVP is a BONUS MODE", which is the very first sentence in its rules.

5. After 2.5 patch, BIAV removes emotes rewards which really works in PVP.

6. After 2.5 patch, BIAV puts rewards what are valuable only in PVE mode.

You need 229/112 to max one awakener.

and progression materials should not be gated behind scoring high

I think priority are:
1) Removing traphase rewards or making them 'play' instead of 'win'
2) moving down the leaderboard rewards or making it score based instead of rank based
3) possibly bringing back old madness pdive

Don't like doing comparisons, but Star Rail made a Whale mode that you have 3 "knight" fights and a "king" fight.
It's subjective, but I think the mode would be awesome for me **IF** it didn't had crafting materials for highest rank on all knights (king is just cosmetics).
For quite a long time I got battle+monthly pass, the moment I, as spender, with good characters and relics wasn't able to max all 3 knight and that was pretty much the trigger to make me stop paying for the game because it felt predatory.
Should have been this patch I think
the event info does say 'tasks can only be completed during the activity period' so I think purchase rewards not being retroactive is intentional rather than a bug
To me gamemodes like the spiral abyss-likes, have always felt unachievable in gacha games. I guess I'm way too much of a casual, to be a metric for people who mainly play to A5 or leaderboard grind.

I get the feeling that BIAV is reluctant to buff Wanda, since at one point she was a meta-defining Awakener in Arc 1. Which is understandable of course; but as a Wanda main, I have to say her AA leaves a lot to be desired, to the point it's basically irrelevant.

Strong Awakeners like Castor get absurdly powerful AAs, whereas Wanda, who's seen by many as too niche and generally weak, gets something that feels like an afterthought.

Now I'm not of the opinion that she should have amazingly good Counter generation, as having her rely on another unit to generate permanent or temporary Counter is perfectly valid as a design decision, and one that I can even agree with.

I also don't think an AA should fix core issues with an Awakener's kit, but it probably should either boost what they're already good at, or shore up a little of their drawbacks. Currently, Wanda's AA does neither.

What I'd propose would be instead of buffing her Counter generation, to instead buff her AA so that after being Roused, Keeper of the Lost and Spine Needle Chains' Leap effects don't consume Dreamlure stacks anymore, and possibly doubling the effect of Strength Down, further buffing her already good debuff stacking.

Additionally, as a baseline buff to her kit, perhaps Keeper of the Lost could apply 1 turn Weakness, (Maybe 2 turns of Weakness with Leap.) to make the card feel more reasonable to play with such a high cost.

Hopefully these adjustments are seen as reasonable, and are considered by the balance team. Thanks for your time!
It's supposed to switch to a weaker attack after if its summons die, but if you kill them with Arachne's Final Verdict it doesn't change.

Arachne's start of combat voice line is long enough that the text box is too large, and partially blocks the Annihilation button. Because you can't click through the text box, this means you have to wait until the line completely finishes or click in exactly the right spot at the very top corner of the icon in order to use Annihilate before she finishes speaking. Please adjust so that the player can click Annihilate through the text box.
the area where the text box blocks clicks is slightly larger than the text itself, so most of the annihilate button ends up covered
wanda is one of the best (and my personal favorite) designs in the game and i would love for her to get some love

Maybe I’m just reading the wording wrong, but when I see:

> Grants Shield and Temporary DMG Amplification +75%

and then Arachne E1 says:

> Arachne’s Exalt grants +25% Temporary DMG Amplification. For every point of Realm Mastery, the Temporary DMG Amplification additionally +0.25%.

I would expect rank 1 to become:

`75% + 25% + RM * 0.25%`

But in practice it seems to behave more like:

`75% * (1.25 + RM * 0.0025)`

Example at rank 1 with 24 Realm Mastery:

Expected from wording:
`75% + 25% + 24 * 0.25% = 106%`

Observed/math result (rounding not included):
`75% * (1.25 + 24 * 0.0025) = 98.25%`

This only matters at lower ranks, because at rank 6 the base value is 100%, which gives both formulas the same result.
And perhaps losing less than 10% dmg amp isnt a big deal..

But I was a little bit confused when trying to do the math for my DB because her skill was not maxed out, so I wanted to ask whether this is intended or a wording/calculation issue.
